Cao Xie

TheLamentedPrinceofZan[贊哀王] was[Cao] Xie. He died early. In the fifth year of theera [231], he was posthumously enfeoffed with the posthumous title of thePrematureDukeofJing[經殤公]. In the second year of theera [234], the posthumous title was changed.1In the third year [235], his son, thePrematurePrince[殤王],[Cao] Xun, succeeded him. In the third year of the Jingchu era [239], the households were increased to five hundred, joining with the previous for a total of three thousand households. In the ninth year of the Zhengshi era [248], he died. He had no sons, and the fiefdom wasabolished.•••
That is to say that the location whereCao Xiewas posthumously enfeoffed was changed fromJingtoZan.↩
